m_Item1.QuestionText: Page Designed in HTML is called as ________.
m_Item1.Explanation: A web page or webpage is a document commonly written in HTML (Hypertext Markup Language) that is accessible through the Internet or other networks using an Internet browser. A web page is accessed by entering a URL address and may contain text, graphics, and hyperlinks to other web pages and files.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTML Document can contain ______________.
m_Item1.Explanation: A web page or webpage is a document, commonly written in HTML, that is viewed in an Internet browser.A web page can be accessed by entering a URL address into a browser's address bar.A web page may contain text, graphics, and hyperlinks to other web pages and files.. A web page is often used to provide information to viewers, including pictures or videos to help illustrate important topics.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTML is platform independent.
m_Item1.Explanation:
HTML is platform independent if you use Netescape Foundation Classes (NFC). the view of Microsoft Foundation Classes is different at different browser. So if you write one HTML code then it might be possible that same will not work at other
browser. .

m_Item1.QuestionText: In order to write HTML code we need to have active internet connection, Are you agree with this statement.
m_Item1.Explanation: We can write HTML webpage though we don't have active internet connection

m_Item1.QuestionText: Metadata does not define _________
m_Item1.Explanation:
Explanation: Metadata define character set, title of the document, links, styles, scripts and other meta information. E.g. <!doctype html> <html>
<title> The Code </title>
 <meta charset= “UTF-8”>
</head>
<body>.

m_Item1.QuestionText: State whether the given statement is true or false. !DOCTYPE is case sensitive”.
m_Item1.Explanation: XML is a case sensitive while HTML is case insensitive. So the declaration “!DOCOTYPE” is case sensitive in XML and it is not case sensitive in HTML, we can write the “!DOCTYPE” declaration in both lowercase and uppercase, thus both are valid.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Choose the correct statement.

Options.OptionText: In traditional XHTML close tag for some elements is optional but not encouraged
In traditional HTML close tag for some elements is optional but not encouraged
In both traditional XHTML and HTML close tag for some elements is not optional
In both traditional XHTML and HTML close tag for some elements is optional

m_Item1.Explanation: In XHTML all elements must always be closed and empty elements also be closed. XHTML close tags are not optional neither they are encouraged. HTML is case insensitive and thus closing tags for some elements including empty elements is optional and is ignored by browsers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following XHTML doctype declaration contains all HTML elements and attributes, including presentational and deprecated elements (like font) but not framesets?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The !DOCTYPE declaration is not an HTML tag; it is an instruction to the web browser about what version of HTML the page is written in. The XHTML doctype declaration
 “!DOCTYPE html PUBLIC “-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.0 Strict//EN” “http://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml1/DTD/xhtml1-strict.dtd”” is of XHTML 1.0 strict,
“!DOCTYPE html PUBLIC “-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.0 Transitional//EN” “http://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml1/DTD/xhtml1-transitional.dtd”” is of XHTML 1.0 transitional,
“!DOCTYPE html PUBLIC “-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.0 Frameset//EN” “http://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml1/DTD/xhtml1-frameset.dtd”” is of XHTML 1.0 frameset and
 “<!DOCTYPE html PUBLIC “-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.1//EN” “http://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml11/DTD/xhtml11.dtd”>” is of XHTML 1.1.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a difference between HTML and XHTML?

Options.OptionText: Charset in html is “text/html” where as in xhtml it is “application/xml+xhtml”
Charset in both html and xhtml is “text/html”
Tags and attributes are case-insensitive in HTML but not in XHTML
Special characters must be escaped using character entities in XHTML unlike HTML

m_Item1.Explanation: HTML is case insensitive while XHTML is case sensitive. In XHTML, special characters can be escaped using character entites but not in HTML. Charset in HTML is “text/html” where as it is “application/xml+xhtml” for XHTML.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What indicates the content in file is HTML when delivered on the network.
m_Item1.Explanation: The most common indicator is the file extension .html. The HTTP response header called Content-Type provides more explicit information about the content of the file. For HTML files, the Content-Type header will be set to text/html.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTML and XHTML stands for ______

Options.OptionText: Hyper Text Markup Language and Extensible HyperText Markup Language
Hyper Text Markup Language and Extensible HyperText Marking Language
Hyper Text Marking Language and EXtensible HyperText Marking Language
Hyper Text Marking Language and Extensible HyperText Markup Language

m_Item1.Explanation: HTML stands for “Hyper Text Markup Language” and XHTML stand for “Extensible HyperText Markup Language”.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTML web pages can be read and rendered by _________.
m_Item1.Explanation: HTML pages read and rendered by different web browsers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The time taken by a packet to travel from client to server and then back to the client is called
m_Item1.Explanation: RTT stands for round-trip time. It's a measurement used in networking to quantify the time it takes for a data packet to travel from a source to a destination and back again.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The HTTP request message is sent in _________ part of three-way handshake.
m_Item1.Explanation: The HTTP request message is sent in Third part of three-way handshake. Once the three-way handshake is complete and a reliable connection is established, the client can send the HTTP request message to the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the process of fetching a web page from a server the HTTP request/response takes __________ RTTs.
m_Item1.Explanation: RTT is one round trip from client to server(in request) and from server back to client(in response)

m_Item1.QuestionText: The first line of HTTP request message is called
m_Item1.Explanation: First line is request line which includes HTTP version followed by URL and followed by request method

m_Item1.QuestionText: The values GET, POST, HEAD etc are specified in ____________ of HTTP message
m_Item1.Explanation: It is specified in the method field of request line in the HTTP request message.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The __________ method when used in the method attribute, leaves entity body empty.
m_Item1.Explanation: get method is not associated with any data in the request body.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The HTTP response message leaves out the requested object when ____________ method is used
m_Item1.Explanation: A response to a HEAD method should not have a body. If it has one anyway, that body must be ignored:

m_Item1.QuestionText: Find the oddly matched HTTP status codes
m_Item1.Explanation: Not found is associated with 404 status

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not correct ?

Options.OptionText: Web cache doesn't has its own disk space
Web cache can act both like server and client
Web cache might reduce the response time
Web cache contains copies of recently requested objects

m_Item1.Explanation: Web cache has its own disk space. A web cache is a system designed to store frequently accessed web content, optimizing web browsing performance. It acts as an intermediary between a client (like your web browser) and a server (where the website content resides)

m_Item1.QuestionText: The conditional GET mechanism

Options.OptionText: Imposes conditions on the objects to be requested
Limits the number of response from a server
Helps to keep a cache upto date

m_Item1.Explanation: The conditional GET mechanism is an HTTP optimization technique that allows a client (like a web browser) to efficiently check if a cached copy of a resource on the server is still up-to-date.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is present in both an HTTP request line and a response status line?
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP version is present in request line and status line

m_Item1.QuestionText: Multiple object can be sent over a TCP connection between client and server in
m_Item1.Explanation: In persistent HTTP cab be used to send multiple object over a TCP connection.

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTTP is ________ protocol.
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP is application layer proticol which sits on top of TCP/IP

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the network HTTP resources are located by
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP resources are located by uniform resource identifier.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Dynamic web page

Options.OptionText: is same every time whenever it displays
generates on demand by a program or a request from browser

m_Item1.Explanation: Dynamic web page generates on demand or a request from browser.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a web browser?

Options.OptionText: a program that can display a web page
a program used to view html documents
it enables user to access the resources of internet

m_Item1.Explanation: Web browser is a program to display web page, used to view html documents and to access the resources of internet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Common gateway interface is used to

Options.OptionText: It is a specification that defines how web servers can interact with external programs or scripts to generate dynamic web content.

m_Item1.Explanation: CGI enables web servers to execute programs written in various languages (e.g., C++, Python, Perl) in response to web requests (typically HTTP requests from a browser).

m_Item1.QuestionText: URL stands for
m_Item1.Explanation: Full form of URL is Unifirm Resource Locator. It is a way to identify the resource on the internet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A web cookie is a small piece of data

Options.OptionText: sent from a website and stored in user’s web browser while a user is browsing a website
sent from user and stored in the server while a user is browsing a website
sent from root server to all servers

m_Item1.Explanation: Cookie is a small bit of information stored in browser. It may be sent by web server or may be generated by java script running in the browser.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which one of the following is not used to generate dynamic web pages?
m_Item1.Explanation: All technologies are used on server side to generate dynamic response.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The number of objects in a Web page which consists of 4 jpeg images and HTML text is
m_Item1.Explanation: 4 jpeg images + 1 base HTML file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The default connection type used by HTTP is
m_Item1.Explanation: Persistent default connection type used by HTTP .

m_Item1.QuestionText: HTTP client requests by establishing a __________ connection to a particular port on the server.
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP works on TCP so esablishing connection is done by TCP

m_Item1.QuestionText: FTP server listens for connection on port number
m_Item1.Explanation: Default port for FTP service is 21.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In FTP protocol, client contacts server using ____ as the transport protocol.
m_Item1.Explanation: FTP depends on TCP for estblishing connection.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which mode FTP, the client initiates both the control and data connections.
m_Item1.Explanation: Passive FTP  keeps all communication from client to server:

m_Item1.QuestionText: The file transfer protocol is built on
m_Item1.Explanation: FTP is based on client server architecture.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In file transfer protocol, data transfer can be done in
m_Item1.Explanation: In FTP, data transfer can be done in 3 different mode. i.e. stream,block and compressed mode.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true about HTML 5?

Options.OptionText: HTML5 is the next major revision of the HTML standard superseding HTML 4.01, XHTML 1.0, and XHTML 1.1.
HTML5 is a standard for structuring and presenting content on the World Wide Web.
HTML5 is a cooperation between the World Wide Web Consortium W3C and the Web Hypertext Application Technology Working Group WHATWG.

m_Item1.Explanation: HTML 5 is the current version used for content presentation in the web pages. It is standardized by W3C

m_Item1.QuestionText: Main protocol used in Internet is:
m_Item1.Explanation: TCP/IP is a protocol used in internet connectivity

m_Item1.QuestionText: Who is making the Web standards?
m_Item1.Explanation: WWW Consortium is made web standards.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Server is one who
m_Item1.Explanation: Server means the one who provides service and in case of web server it hosts websites.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is true about doctype declaration?

Options.OptionText: It is an "information" to the browser about what document type to expect.
<!DOCTYPE html> acts as a declaration that this document is an HTML5 document.
DOCTYPE declaration should be in the first line of a webpage.

m_Item1.Explanation: DOCTYPE declaration should be in the first line of a webpage. DOCTYPE is a type declaration that is used to tell web browsers what version of HTML we are using in our webpage/website.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP status code indicates a missing file?
m_Item1.Explanation: 404 Not Found: This status code is used to indicate that the requested resource (file or webpage) could not be found on the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following early networks is considered the precursor to the modern Internet?
m_Item1.Explanation: ARPANET, developed by the United States Department of Defense in the late 1960s, is considered the precursor to the modern Internet. It was the first network to implement packet switching and laid the foundation for internet protocols.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In what year was the World Wide Web invented, providing an accessible interface for using the Internet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989. It provided a user-friendly way to access documents and content over the Internet using hyperlinks and browsers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which protocol, introduced in 1982, is fundamental for linking different networks and the existence of the Internet?
m_Item1.Explanation: TCP/IP, stands for Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol, introduced in 1982, is the protocol that allows diverse computer networks to interconnect and communicate over the Internet.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary function of the Domain Name System (DNS) in the context of how the Internet works?

Options.OptionText: To encrypt data for secure transmission
To translate domain names into IP addresses
To manage the bandwidth of data transmission
To store all the websites available on the Internet

m_Item1.Explanation: The Domain Name System (DNS) is responsible for translating human-friendly domain names (like www.example.com) into IP addresses, which computers use to identify each other on the network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which protocol is primarily responsible for defining how data packets are addressed and routed across networks in the Internet?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Internet Protocol (IP) is responsible for addressing and routing packets of data so they can travel across networks and arrive at the correct destination.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the context of the Internet, what role does an Internet Service Provider (ISP) play?

Options.OptionText: An ISP controls the World Wide Web infrastructure
An ISP sells domain names to users
An ISP provides users access to the Internet
An ISP hosts websites for clients

m_Item1.Explanation: An Internet Service Provider (ISP) is a company that provides users with access to the Internet, allowing devices to connect and communicate over the worldwide network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is used to request data from a specified resource?
m_Item1.Explanation: The GET method is used to retrieve data from a specified resource. It is the default option and should not have any side effects on the data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary function of the DELETE HTTP method?

Options.OptionText: To retrieve metadata about a resource

m_Item1.Explanation: The DELETE method is used to remove a resource from a server. It is idempotent, meaning multiple identical requests should have the same effect as a single request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which status code indicates that the server successfully processed the request, but is returning no content?
m_Item1.Explanation: The HTTP status code 204 No Content is used when the server successfully processes the request but does not return any content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the HTTP status code 301 mean?
m_Item1.Explanation: The HTTP status code 301 indicates a permanent redirect, meaning the requested resource has been permanently moved to a new URL.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which header in an HTTP request is used to specify the server what media type client is sending in the request?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Content-Type' request header is used to specify the media types that are client is sending to the server

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true regarding the Domain Name System (DNS)?

Options.OptionText: DNS translates domain names into IP addresses.
DNS stores only the IP addresses of websites.
DNS is a type of web server that hosts website content.
DNS only works for domain names ending with '.com'.

m_Item1.Explanation: DNS is a hierarchical system that translates user-friendly domain names into machine-friendly IP addresses that computers use to identify each other over the network.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of a top-level domain (TLD) in a domain name?

Options.OptionText: To specify the country of origin of the website.
To represent the domain owner's name.
To categorize the domain names and denote the website's purpose.
To increase security by hiding the website's IP address.

m_Item1.Explanation: A top-level domain (TLD) serves as the last segment of a domain name. It categorizes domain names and often provides insight into the nature of the web entity, such as ‘.com’ for commercial or ‘.edu’ for educational.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the acronym 'TLD' stand for in domain names?
m_Item1.Explanation: TLD stands for Top-Level Domain, which is the last segment of a domain name, after the final dot. Examples include '.com', '.org', and '.net'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a significant improvement from HTTP/1.0 to HTTP/1.1?
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP/1.1 introduced persistent connections, allowing multiple requests and responses between client and server without opening a new connection each time, unlike HTTP/1.0.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a major feature of HTTP/2.0 that distinguishes it from HTTP/1.1?

Options.OptionText: Multiplexing multiple requests over a single connection

m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP/2.0 allows multiplexing multiple requests and responses over a single connection, which helps in reducing latency and enhancing the speed, unlike HTTP/1.1 which has issues with head-of-line blocking.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What was a notable drawback of HTTP/1.0 that led to further development in HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2.0?
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP/1.0 did not support connection reuse, leading to inefficient use of network resources, as each request/response pair required a new connection. This led to improvements in HTTP/1.1 with persistent connections and further advancements in HTTP/2.0.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is used to request data from a specified resource?
m_Item1.Explanation: GET is used to request data from a specified resource without modifying it.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is idempotent, meaning multiple identical requests should have the same effect as a single request?
m_Item1.Explanation: PUT is an idempotent method; making multiple identical requests results in the same effect as a single request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is used to submit data to be processed to a specified resource?
m_Item1.Explanation: POST is used to submit data to a specified resource for processing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the HEAD HTTP method do?
m_Item1.Explanation: HEAD is used to get the headers (metadata) of a resource, without the body.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is typically used to delete a resource from the server?
m_Item1.Explanation: DELETE is used to remove a specified resource from the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method would you use to replace a resource at a specific URL?
m_Item1.Explanation: PUT replaces all current representations of the target resource with the uploaded content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is not supposed to alter the state of the server?
m_Item1.Explanation: GET is used for requesting data without making any changes on the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary use of the DELETE HTTP method?
m_Item1.Explanation: DELETE is used to remove a resource identified by a URI on the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method should be used to ensure a request to update a resource is idempotent?
m_Item1.Explanation: PUT is idempotent, meaning multiple requests will have the same effect as a single request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When using the POST method, how are parameters typically sent?
m_Item1.Explanation: POST sends parameters in the request body, unlike GET which appends them to the URL.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following HTTP methods is safe and idempotent?
m_Item1.Explanation: GET is both safe (does not change the resource state) and idempotent (same result for multiple requests).

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a major difference between POST and PUT methods?

Options.OptionText: POST is idempotent, PUT is not
POST is used for updates, PUT is for new data
POST creates or updates, PUT only updates
PUT is idempotent, POST is not

m_Item1.Explanation: PUT is idempotent, meaning multiple identical requests have the same effect, while POST is not. POST can create or process data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method can be used to check if a resource exists without fetching its entire content?
m_Item1.Explanation: HEAD requests only the headers that would be returned if the GET method was used, without the body.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When is the PUT method typically not used?

Options.OptionText: To create a resource at a specified URI

m_Item1.Explanation: PUT is mainly used to create or update resources at a specified URI, not for submitting user data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is specifically designed to upload a representation of the target resource?
m_Item1.Explanation: PUT is used to upload a complete representation of the resource, either creating or replacing it at the specified URI.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the HTTP Status Code 404 represent?
m_Item1.Explanation: The HTTP Status Code 404 indicates that the requested resource could not be found on the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP Status Code indicates that the request has been fulfilled and resulted in a new resource being created?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 201 Created status code indicates that the request has been successful and a new resource has been created as a result.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is indicated by the HTTP Status Code 500?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 500 Internal Server Error status code indicates that the server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which status code represents that the request method is not supported by the server?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 405 Method Not Allowed status code indicates that the request method is known by the server but is not supported by the target resource.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP Status Code is used when the server cannot find a resource that matches the URI?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 404 Not Found status code indicates that the server can not find the requested resource.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the HTTP Status Code 302 indicate?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 302 Found status code indicates that the resource requested has been temporarily moved to a different URI.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When a server responds with a 204 status code, what does it mean?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 204 No Content status code indicates that the server has successfully processed the request, but is not returning any content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What HTTP Status Code represents that the resource has moved permanently to a new URI?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 301 Moved Permanently status code indicates that the resource requested has been permanently moved to a new URL specified in the Location header.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP Status Code suggests that a request must be completed by following the URI given in the Location header?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 303 See Other status code indicates that the response can be found under a different URI and should be retrieved using a GET method on that resource.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the HTTP Status Code 401 indicate?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 401 Unauthorized status code indicates that the request has not been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the target resource.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is meant by the stateless nature of the HTTP protocol?

Options.OptionText: The server maintains session state for each client automatically.
Each HTTP request is independent and does not require information from previous requests.
The server keeps track of the state using cookies.
HTTP connections are encrypted and secure by default.

m_Item1.Explanation: The stateless nature of HTTP means that each request is independent and the server does not retain information about past requests. This requires additional mechanisms like cookies or session storage to maintain state.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a consequence of HTTP being stateless?

Options.OptionText: Increased server workload due to maintaining client session states.
Simplified server-side design as no context is retained between requests.
All HTTP requests must be encrypted by default.
HTTP can only support a limited number of clients at the same time.

m_Item1.Explanation: The stateless nature of HTTP simplifies server design because the server doesn’t have to keep track of client interactions between requests, leading to more scalable and flexible web applications.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can the stateless nature of HTTP be managed to maintain user sessions?

Options.OptionText: By embedding hidden fields within HTML forms.
By encrypting all HTTP requests and responses.
By using IP address tracking to recall client sessions.

m_Item1.Explanation: Hidden fields in HTML forms can be used to maintain session information across multiple requests in HTTP, despite its stateless nature. Other common methods include utilizing cookies and URL tokens.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the 'S' in HTTPS stand for?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'S' in HTTPS stands for 'Secure', indicating that the protocol provides a secure, encrypted connection between the client and the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of HTTPS?

Options.OptionText: To compress data for faster transmission
To encrypt data in transit for security

m_Item1.Explanation: HTTPS encrypts the data in transit between the user's browser and the server, making it secure from eavesdropping or tampering by attackers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which protocol is used for encryption in HTTPS?
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTPS uses SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) or its successor, TLS (Transport Layer Security), to encrypt the data being transmitted.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why is it important for websites to implement HTTPS?

Options.OptionText: For better SEO and user trust

m_Item1.Explanation: Implementing HTTPS increases user trust due to the secured connection, and search engines like Google give preference to HTTPS websites, improving SEO.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a visual indicator that a browser is using an HTTPS connection?
m_Item1.Explanation: When a browser is using an HTTPS connection, it often displays a padlock icon in the address bar to indicate a secure connection is established.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which protocol is primarily used for retrieving webpages from a web server?
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) is the foundation of data communication for the World Wide Web, used primarily for retrieving web pages from web servers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the three-tier architecture of web applications, what does the presentation tier typically handle?
m_Item1.Explanation: The presentation tier is responsible for the user interface and user interaction, essentially what the users see and interact with.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following best describes the role of a reverse proxy in web architecture?

Options.OptionText: Acts as a caching mechanism for client requests
Directly connects clients to external database servers
Returns responses from backups if the server fails
Routes client requests to the appropriate server and handles incoming traffic

m_Item1.Explanation: A reverse proxy sits in front of web servers and forwards client requests to the appropriate backend server, thus helping in load balancing, traffic management, and improving security.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following accurately describes the role of a web server like IIS or Apache Server?

Options.OptionText: A web server is primarily used to execute client-side scripts.
A web server is responsible for storing user login credentials securely.
A web server handles HTTP requests and serves web pages to clients.
A web server is a tool for designing dynamic web content.

m_Item1.Explanation: Web servers like IIS (Internet Information Services) and Apache are responsible for processing HTTP requests and serving web pages to clients. They act as a mediator between the client and the hosted web content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a feature commonly associated with Apache Server?
m_Item1.Explanation: Apache Server does not have built-in support for Microsoft ASP.NET. This feature is more associated with Microsoft's IIS (Internet Information Services) than with Apache.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary difference between IIS and Apache web servers?

Options.OptionText: IIS is open-source, while Apache is proprietary.
Apache is more commonly used on Linux, whereas IIS is not limited to any specific OS.
IIS supports ASP.NET natively while Apache primarily uses modules for programming language support.
Apache is used less frequently than IIS across the internet.

m_Item1.Explanation: IIS (Internet Information Services) natively supports ASP.NET because it is developed by Microsoft. Apache, on the other hand, is known for its modular system that allows support for different programming languages, typically through additional modules.
